# 'branch restack' when trunk is checked out in another worktree.
# A branch based on trunk should restack successfully
# even if trunk is checked out in a different worktree.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2026-03-29T14:00:00Z'

# setup
cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'
gs repo init

# create a stack: main -> feature1 -> feature2
git add feature1.txt
gs bc feature1 -m 'Add feature1'
git add feature2.txt
gs bc feature2 -m 'Add feature2'

# create a worktree for feature1
# (feature2 stays in the main repo)
git worktree add ../wt-feature1 feature1

# go back to main and make a change that requires restacking
gs trunk
cp $WORK/extra/main-update.txt main-update.txt
git add main-update.txt
git commit -m 'Update main'

# go to the feature1 worktree and restack
cd ../wt-feature1
gs branch restack
stderr 'feature1: restacked'

# verify the branch graph from wt-feature1
git graph --branches
cmp stdout $WORK/golden/graph-after-restack.txt

# now go back to main repo and restack feature2
cd ../repo
gs bco feature2
gs branch restack
stderr 'feature2: restacked'

git graph --branches
cmp stdout $WORK/golden/graph-final.txt

-- repo/feature1.txt --
feature1
-- repo/feature2.txt --
feature2
-- extra/main-update.txt --
main update
-- golden/graph-after-restack.txt --
* f5facc1 (HEAD -> feature1) Add feature1
* 4bced44 (main) Update main
| * 9726f03 (feature2) Add feature2
| * d1dd13f Add feature1
|/  
* e2cefa6 Initial commit
-- golden/graph-final.txt --
* 40815b9 (HEAD -> feature2) Add feature2
* f5facc1 (feature1) Add feature1
* 4bced44 (main) Update main
* e2cefa6 Initial commit
